status: P1 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 6 / BIOLOGIC OR MIXTURE IDENTITY BLOCK ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Porcine-brain-derived peptide hydrolysate medicinal product family
mechanism_summary: A complex, manufacturing-dependent mixture proposed to influence neurotrophic, neuronal-survival and neural-recovery pathways; the active component or components are not defined as one canonical peptide.
evidence_maturity: Multiple randomised clinical studies and systematic reviews across stroke, dementia, traumatic brain injury and other neurological contexts, with heterogeneous findings and product-specific evidence.
regulatory_headline: REGIONAL MEDICINAL PRODUCT CONTEXTS EXIST — NO FDA, UK OR CENTRAL EU AUTHORISATION CLAIM
certification_blockers: Current national authorisation and label capture
Supplier cold-chain, authenticity, batch and sterility evidence
closure_class: PORCINE-BRAIN HYDROLYSATE MIXTURE / BRAND AND BATCH COMPARABILITY
closure_state: PARTIAL CLOSURE — BRANDED CEREBROLYSIN TRIAL MATERIAL AND CURRENT CLINICAL PROGRAMMES IDENTIFIED; CBL60 FORMULATION, MANUFACTURER AND COMPARABILITY OPEN
authoritative_product_boundary: ClinicalTrials.gov records describe manufacturer-controlled Cerebrolysin as an intravenous porcine-brain-derived preparation containing low-molecular-weight peptides and free amino acids, generally measured by volume. The CBL60 catalogue presentation of 60 mg x 6 vials does not establish branded-product identity or formulation.
critical_closure_controls: CBL60 is not assumed to be EVER Pharma Cerebrolysin
A 60 mg catalogue strength is not comparable to trial volumes without composition data
Stroke, dementia and injury evidence is product- and indication-specific
No universal FDA, MHRA or central EU authorisation claim
No neurological treatment, infusion, dosing or reconstitution protocol
